# Liu Yuchen (Lyrica)

> Founder of Φ(fight) Research — independent research collective for physical-world AI fighting. HKUST EE+AI sophomore. Four sole/co-first-author papers on mechanistic interpretability and methodology for vision-language-action models under double-blind review at NeurIPS 2026 (the technical groundwork that Φ-Arena combat evaluation builds on).

## Identity

- **Full name:** Liu Yuchen
- **Alternate names:** Lyrica, kaminoguo (GitHub handle)
- **Pronouns:** he/him
- **Role:** Founder, Φ(fight) Research (May 2026 – present)
- **Affiliation:** Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST), EE + AI dual major, sophomore
- **Email:** yuchen@φ.monster (technical: yuchen@xn--7xa.monster)

- **Personal site:** https://lyrica.φ.monster (technical: https://lyrica.xn--7xa.monster)
- **Φ org:** https://φ.monster

## Current focus — Φ(fight) Research

Founded May 2026 with Han Muchen. Independent collective studying what happens when physical AIs fight each other in shared physical space. Anti-VC, open-publication-only, ten-year program.

Three papers in progress for ICLR 2027 under Φ(fight) Research:

1. **Φ-Arena (co-led with Han Muchen)** — first open benchmark for VLA-vs-VLA combat evaluation under contact-rich physics, across MuJoCo / Isaac Sim / Genesis, with full pairwise matchup across OpenVLA, OpenVLA-OFT, π₀, and SmolVLA
2. **Mechanistic interpretability of self-play policies (Liu Yuchen lead)** — circuit-level analysis of exploit-prone subnetworks emerging during adversarial self-play training in Φ-Arena
3. **Energy-bounded adversarial games (Han Muchen lead)** — empirical characterization of how physical resource constraints reshape emergent strategy distributions in combat self-play

All artifacts open-source on HuggingFace and GitHub at ICLR submission (September 2026).

## Pre-Φ research — four papers under NeurIPS 2026 double-blind review

Three sole-author, one co-first. This is the technical groundwork that informed Φ's methodology (activation-intervention toolkit, closed-loop VLA evaluation infrastructure, deployment-failure auditing) — Yuchen's pre-Φ work on the interpretability instrument that Φ-Arena's combat analysis applies to self-play policies.

Titles and anonymous repository identifiers withheld until NeurIPS 2026 decisions in September 2026 to preserve double-blind review integrity. Full information available on request after the review period.

Topic descriptions (review-safe):

1. **Sole author.** Mechanistic interpretability of transformer language models — shows that a class of widely-used spectral diagnostics on hidden states is systematically biased by an input-invariant high-variance direction; proposes a one-line correction.
2. **Sole author.** Activation-ablation methodology for Vision-Language-Action models — the standard ablation primitive overstates per-layer importance; introduces a corrected protocol implemented as forward-hook context managers, with closed-loop validation in a robotic simulator.
3. **Sole author.** Cross-view attention analysis in VLAs — quantitative framework relating attention partition to behavioral multi-view fusion.
4. **Co-first author.** Deployment-failure audit suite for VLAs — mutually-exclusive failure-mode taxonomy with calibrated confidence intervals.

## Pre-research projects

- **Squirrel** (Dec 2025 – Feb 2026): AI memory layer for coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex). Rust daemon + MCP server + Python service for LLM-based memory extraction. Archived at ~1,000+ GitHub stars when pivoted to research.
- **OfferI** (Feb 2025 – Dec 2025): AI study-abroad school selection agent for international students. Closed.
- **Xiaoniao** (Aug – Sep 2025): Cross-platform translation tool, Go (Chrome extension + Windows app, three AI provider integrations).

## Technical stack

- **Languages:** Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go (proficient)
- **Research tools:** PyTorch + HuggingFace Transformers / datasets / accelerate
- **Hands-on VLA models:** OpenVLA family, π₀, X-VLA, SmolVLA
- **Simulators:** MuJoCo, Isaac Sim, Genesis, LIBERO
- **Statistical methods:** Wilson confidence intervals, cluster bootstrap, activation interventions, forward-hook analysis
- **Hardware:** LeKiwi mobile manipulator, dual-arm bimanual setups, embedded systems

## Education

Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Sep 2024 – 2029 (expected). Electronic Engineering + Artificial Intelligence dual major. Top 3 in COMP6211 (PhD-level AI Startup Course), Summer 2025.

## What I'm not

- Not affiliated with any big AI lab (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind, etc.)
- Not raising VC, not building a startup
- Not pursuing a PhD yet — open question for Year 2-3
- Not primarily a "mechinterp researcher" — the interpretability work is the diagnostic instrument Φ-Arena combat analysis applies; the research identity is physical AI combat
